docs.title: Petstore API
docs.text: A production-ready Python backend API implementing the Petstore OpenAPI 3.0 spec, built with FastAPI and deployable to AWS EKS.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Start the API in in-memory mode (no external dependencies):

docker compose up

Then test the health endpoint:

curl http://localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Full Petstore API (pets, store, users)
  • Three runtime modes: in-memory, local PostgreSQL, AWS RDS
  • Structured JSON logging with correlation IDs
  • API key authentication with JWT-ready interface
  • Deployable via Docker locally and Helm to AWS EKS

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: The Petstore API implements the Petstore OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

docs.title: Interactive Documentation
docs.text:
When running locally, visit:

  • Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs
  • ReDoc: http://localhost:8000/redoc
  • OpenAPI JSON: http://localhost:8000/openapi.json

docs.title: Pet
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /api/v1/pet Add a new pet PUT /api/v1/pet Update an existing pet GET /api/v1/pet/findByStatus Find pets by status GET /api/v1/pet/findByTags Find pets by tags GET /api/v1/pet/{petId} Get pet by ID POST /api/v1/pet/{petId} Update pet with form data DELETE /api/v1/pet/{petId} Delete a pet POST /api/v1/pet/{petId}/uploadFile Upload pet image

docs.title: Store
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /api/v1/store/inventory Returns pet inventories by status POST /api/v1/store/order Place an order GET /api/v1/store/order/{orderId} Find order by ID DELETE /api/v1/store/order/{orderId} Delete order

docs.title: User
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /api/v1/user Create user POST /api/v1/user/createWithList Create users with list GET /api/v1/user/login Log user in GET /api/v1/user/logout Log user out GET /api/v1/user/{username} Get user by username PUT /api/v1/user/{username} Update user DELETE /api/v1/user/{username} Delete user

docs.title: Health
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /health Service health check (unauthenticated)

docs.title: Service Layers
docs.text:
graph TD
    Client --> APIGateway["API Gateway / ALB"]
    APIGateway --> FastAPI["FastAPI App"]
    FastAPI --> CorrelationMiddleware["Correlation ID Middleware"]
    CorrelationMiddleware --> AuthMiddleware["Auth Middleware (X-API-Key)"]
    AuthMiddleware --> Router["API Router /api/v1/"]
    Router --> PetService["PetService"]
    Router --> OrderService["OrderService"]
    Router --> UserService["UserService"]
    PetService --> PetRepo["PetRepository (Protocol)"]
    OrderService --> OrderRepo["OrderRepository (Protocol)"]
    UserService --> UserRepo["UserRepository (Protocol)"]
    PetRepo --> MemoryImpl["Memory Implementation"]
    PetRepo --> PostgresImpl["PostgreSQL Implementation"]
    PostgresImpl --> RDS["AWS RDS PostgreSQL"]

docs.title: CI/CD Flow
docs.text:
graph LR
    PR["Pull Request"] --> CI["CI: lint + type-check + test"]
    CI --> Build["Build Docker Image"]
    Build --> ECR["Push to AWS ECR"]
    ECR --> Dev["Deploy to Dev EKS"]
    Dev --> Staging["Deploy to Staging (manual)"]
    Staging --> Prod["Deploy to Prod (manual + approval)"]

docs.title: Component Overview
docs.text: Layer Component Responsibility Transport FastAPI / Uvicorn HTTP routing, request/response handling Middleware CorrelationId, Auth Cross-cutting concerns API v1 routers Endpoint definitions, request validation Service PetService, etc. Business logic Repository Memory / Postgres Data persistence abstraction ORM SQLAlchemy 2.x async Database mapping Config Pydantic Settings Environment-based configuration

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial scaffold: FastAPI application with full Petstore API implementation
  • In-memory, local PostgreSQL, and AWS RDS storage modes
  • Correlation ID middleware
  • API key authentication middleware
  • Structured JSON logging with structlog
  • Repository pattern with Protocol-based abstractions
  • Docker and Docker Compose support
  • Helm chart for Kubernetes deployment
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows
  • Comprehensive test suite (unit, integration, system, e2e)
  • MkDocs documentation site

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All settings are loaded from environment variables. Copy .env.example to .env for local development.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Default Description STORAGE_MODE memory Runtime mode: memory | local | cloud API_KEY dev-api-key Required API key for authentication DATABASE_URL "" PostgreSQL connection URL (required for non-memory mode) APP_ENV dev Application environment: dev | staging | prod DEBUG false Enable debug mode LOG_LEVEL INFO Logging level DB_POOL_SIZE 5 SQLAlchemy connection pool size DB_MAX_OVERFLOW 10 SQLAlchemy max overflow connections DB_POOL_TIMEOUT 30 SQLAlchemy pool timeout (seconds)

docs.title: Storage Modes
docs.text:
• memory: All data is held in-process. No external dependencies. Perfect for local dev and unit tests.
  • local: Connects to a local PostgreSQL via DATABASE_URL.
  • cloud: Connects to AWS RDS PostgreSQL.

docs.title: Docker (Local)
docs.text:
# In-memory mode
docker compose up

# With local PostgreSQL
docker compose -f docker-compose.postgres.yml up

docs.title: Fly.io
docs.text: Fly.io is the primary deployment platform.

docs.title: PR / Review Apps
docs.text:
Review apps are deployed automatically for branches prefixed with fly/. The app is torn down when the PR is closed.

  • Trigger: open or push to a fly/* branch PR
  • Config: .fly/pr/fly.toml

docs.title: Dev
docs.text:
# Manual deploy (uses latest GHCR image by default)
gh workflow run deploy-fly-dev.yml

# Deploy a specific version
gh workflow run deploy-fly-dev.yml -f version=v1.2.3
  • Config: .fly/dev/fly.toml
  • Auto-triggered after the GHCR image is published on merge to main

docs.title: Staging
docs.text:
# Deploy a specific version to staging
gh workflow run "Deploy to Fly.io Staging" -f version=v1.2.3
  • Config: .fly/staging/fly.toml
  • Manual trigger via workflow_dispatch

docs.title: CI/CD
docs.text:
Deployments are managed via GitHub Actions:

Environment Trigger Workflow PR review app Open/push to fly/* PR deploy-fly-pr.yml Dev Auto after GHCR image publish (main) deploy-fly-dev.yml Staging Manual workflow_dispatch deploy-fly-staging.yml

Note: AWS (ECS/EKS) and Kubernetes (Helm) deployment is planned but not yet active.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.14+
  • Docker & Docker Compose
  • uv package manager

Install uv:

curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

docs.title: Local Development (In-Memory Mode)
docs.text:
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/RamonAlcantaraArceo/petstore-api.git
cd petstore-api

# 2. Copy env file and set your API key
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and set API_KEY=your-dev-key

# 3. Start the API
docker compose up

# 4. Test the health endpoint
curl http://localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Local Development (Python)
docs.text:
# Create a Python 3.14 environment and install dependencies
uv sync --python 3.14 --extra dev

# Set environment variables
export STORAGE_MODE=memory
export API_KEY=dev-api-key

# Start the server
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Unit tests
uv run pytest tests/unit/

# Integration tests
uv run pytest tests/integration/

# System tests
uv run pytest tests/system/

# All tests with coverage
uv run pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ tests/system/ --cov=app

docs.title: Test Layers
docs.text: Layer Directory Description Unit tests/unit/ Service layer, mocked repositories Integration tests/integration/ HTTP-level, in-memory or testcontainers System tests/system/ Multi-step workflow tests E2E tests/e2e/ Live service smoke tests Performance performance/ pytest-benchmark (manual only)

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Unit tests only
pytest tests/unit/

# Integration tests
pytest tests/integration/

# All CI tests with coverage
pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ tests/system/ --cov=app

# E2E against staging (requires E2E_BASE_URL)
E2E_BASE_URL=https://staging.example.com pytest tests/e2e/

docs.title: Coverage
docs.text:
Minimum 80% coverage is enforced in CI. Generate a report:

pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ tests/system/ \
  --cov=app --cov-report=html
open htmlcov/index.html
